title: Intensely Spicy Middle Eastern Lamb Shakshuka
description: A tangy and snappy post-workout Middle Eastern dish featuring ground cumin and lamb, perfect for a quick and flavorful meal. This gluten-free-friendly shakshuka is made with a spicy lamb marinade and served with crusty bread.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, ground cumin, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat and add the lamb mixture, cooking until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the chopped onions and bell peppers to the skillet and cook until they are softened.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, covered, for 10 minutes.
Make 4 wells in the lamb mixture and crack an egg into each well.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and bake for 15 minutes, or until the eggs are cooked to desired doneness.
Remove the skillet from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before serving.
